parsed_review.summary:
CP00-378 is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only pack for the RP12.P05 implementation slice (10 units, RP12.P05.M03.S09-S18, risk A). Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only — no commands run, no writes.

Scope & distribution: pack_id, risk_class A, unit_count 10, range, upstream CP00-377, next CP00-379, next_subphase S19 all agree across manifest, closeout-pack-plan.json, review-baseline, registry binding (BILLING_CORE_CP378_PACK_BINDING), and contract current_pack. Deliverable distribution implementation:3, security_audit:2, fixture:1, test:3, hermes_evidence:1 (=10) is identical in prompt, manifest delivery_counts, cp378_requirements.deliverable_counts, and the plan's included_units. The 10 row titles (Cross-tenant case … Claude missing-test prompt) map exactly to S09-S18 and match registry required_section_rows, contract, and the service case-set.

Implementation: service.js defines the case-set and descriptor builders; validators.js defines coverage, slice, Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and closeout handoff — following the established CP377 pattern. All 7 required_public_exports are present and auto re-exported through index.js (`export *`), so the unmodified index.js is correct. model.test.js asserts coverage.valid, slice.valid, descriptor-only rows (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false), authority boundary, handoff target/subphase, and no-write attestation flags. command-evidence.json records node --check/--test, rp12:billing-core:validate, npm test, validate, fullplan/goal/control-plane validates, build, and git diff --check all at exit 0; Hermes gate H12 passed.

Boundaries: extensive no-write/no-real-data attestations (descriptor_only, no billing/proforma/invoice/write-down runtime, no DB/object-storage/audit writes, no real fixture data, no Hermes runtime receipt, no leak guards). Authority boundary preserved — claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false. Hermes patterns appear only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (H12.CP00-378…/receipt shape), not product runtime. production_ready and runtime_ready are correctly false; the pack does not over-claim readiness.

Review hardening (CP178+): auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, is_error=false, 1434-byte stdout); prompt 2173 bytes < 25600; tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob (no forbidden tools); summary-only review correctly prohibited due to security/audit deliverables, so source was inspected directly; forbidden_review_evidence list (auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing fields) is encoded in requirements.

No P0/P1/P2 defects found. One non-blocking P3 note that the post-review artifacts are still pending by design. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer; final enterprise-trust/runtime approval remains a human gate and is not asserted here.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts pending by design; production_ready correctly remains false
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts and each unit's evidence_refs list claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-378/. This is expected at the read_only_claude_run_raw_capture stage of the hardening_sequence: those artifacts are produced downstream from this review (normalize -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection). Consistent with this, manifest status is 'implemented_pending_review', pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending', production_ready=false, and runtime_ready=false. command-evidence.json notes the post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack-plan:generate, closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) are still to be executed before commit. Not a defect in the implementation under review.
findings.recommendation: Generate the three post-review artifacts from this accepted review, run the post-review gated validation commands, and keep production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false until human final approval opens runtime in a later pack. No change to the descriptor implementation is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected/by-design at the read_only review stage; non-blocking. Confirms the pack is not over-claiming readiness and that Claude is not acting as final approver.
